name: geojson (GeoJSON Python Utilities)

performance.notes: Pure Python; fast for small feature collections; large collections should use Fiona/Shapely

agent_readiness.notes: GeoJSON RFC 7946 Python library. from geojson import Point, LineString, Polygon, Feature, FeatureCollection, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, GeometryCollection. p = Point((-122.4194, 37.7749)). f = Feature(geometry=p, properties={'name': 'San Francisco'}). fc = FeatureCollection([f]). import geojson; geojson.dumps(fc, sort_keys=True). geojson.loads(json_str). f.is_valid. geojson.utils.generate_random('Point'). Validation: geojson.validation. BBox: Feature(geometry=p, bbox=[-180, -90, 180, 90]). Coordinate reference system (CRS) support. Integrates with Shapely, Fiona, geopandas via __geo_interface__.

gotchas: Longitude first, latitude second — GeoJSON spec (opposite of many GPS APIs)
No spatial operations — use Shapely for intersections, buffering, etc.
Large files should be streamed with fiona or pyogrio
Properties are freeform dict — no schema validation
best_when: Creating and serializing GeoJSON structures for APIs and map services
Integrating with web mapping APIs (Mapbox, Leaflet, MapTiler)
avoid_when: Large files / bulk processing (use Fiona or pyogrio)
summary: geojson provides Python GeoJSON (RFC 7946) construction and serialization. Point((-122.4, 37.7)), Feature(geometry, properties), FeatureCollection([features]). geojson.dumps() / geojson.loads(). is_valid. BSD-3 by Jazzband. Best for creating GeoJSON for APIs and map services. Longitude-first (GeoJSON spec). No spatial operations — use Shapely. No type annotations. Lightweight alternative when geopandas is too heavy.
